title: Functional Constipation
chief_complaint: My daughter hasn't had a bowel movement in 10 days. When she goes, it's huge, hard, and painful. She's holding it in - she crosses her legs, hides in the corner, and refuses to use the toilet. She's leaking small amounts of stool in her underwear. She was potty trained but now she's having accidents.

physical_exam.Abdomen: Distended. Fecal mass palpable in left lower quadrant (descending/sigmoid colon). Soft, non-tender.
physical_exam.Rectal: Normal sphincter tone. Hard stool in rectal vault. No fissure. No blood. Empty after digital disimpaction.
physical_exam.Growth: Normal growth curve. No failure to thrive (differentiates from organic causes).
physical_exam.Neurologic: Normal lower extremity reflexes and perianal sensation (rules out spinal cord pathology).

hidden_data.abdominal_xr: Fecal loading throughout colon with significant stool burden in rectum. No signs of obstruction. Normal bowel gas pattern.
hidden_data.allergy: No cow's milk protein allergy symptoms

correct_diagnosis: Functional Constipation with Fecal Impaction and Encopresis (Overflow Incontinence)
key_learning_points: Functional constipation = most common GI disorder in children (3-5% of pediatric visits). Diagnosed by Rome IV criteria: ≥2 of: Straining, hard stools, sensation of incomplete evacuation, <3 stools/week, fecal incontinence >1/week after toilet trained

Encopresis = overflow incontinence from chronic constipation. Liquid stool leaks around impacted fecal mass into underwear. NOT behavioral - it's physiologic. Child loses sensation of rectal fullness from chronic distention
Painful defecation → stool withholding behavior (leg crossing, hiding) → prolonged rectal stasis → water absorption → hard, large stool → more pain → vicious cycle

RED FLAGS (suggest organic cause): Delayed meconium passage (>48h - Hirschsprung), Failure to thrive, Bilious vomiting, Abdominal distension with tenderness, Abnormal neurologic exam (spinal cord lesion), Sacral dimple/hair tuft, Tight empty rectum (Hirschsprung), Blood in stool (without anal fissure)

Treatment: 1) DISIMPACTION: PEG 3350 1-1.5g/kg/day ×3 days or enemas (mineral oil, phosphate). 2) MAINTENANCE: PEG 3350 0.4-0.8g/kg/day ×2-6 months. 3) BEHAVIORAL: Scheduled toilet sits after meals for 5-10 minutes, reward system (stickers), foot support on toilet, avoid punishment. 4) DIET: Increase fiber, fluids. Prognosis: 50% resolve in 1 year, 30% have symptoms into adulthood
